claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
circumstances: Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. My son Alex, he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand a bit more about your circumstances.

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, does Alex live with you, and are you the person who looks after him and pays for things like food, clothes, and other costs for raising him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex doesn't actually live with me. He lives with his mum. So she's the one who looks after him day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I understand Alex lives with his mum who looks after him day-to-day.

In this situation, it's important to know: Is Alex's mum currently claiming Child Benefit for him, or do you know if she plans to claim it?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, his mum is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

Just to confirm - are you asking about claiming Child Benefit for Alex yourself, even though he lives with his mum and she's already claiming it? Or are you inquiring for a different reason?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm asking about claiming it myself. I wanted to know if I could claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Before I can give you a final decision, let me read the official guidance to ensure I provide you with accurate information about your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I now have the information I need to assess your eligibility.

One final question to make sure I have the complete picture: Are you contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep? This could include things like paying for clothes, food, presents, pocket money, or contributing to his accommodation - and would need to be at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (or the equivalent in kind)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on the Child Benefit rules and your circumstances, I can now give you a decision.

**You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

According to the Child Benefit rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. To qualify, you need to be "responsible" for the child, which means either:
1. You live with them, OR
2. You're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - such as food, clothes, or pocket money

In your situation:
- Alex lives with his mum, not with you
- You're not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep
- His mum is already claiming Child Benefit for him

Since Alex doesn't live with you and you're not making financial contributions towards his care, you're not considered "responsible" for him under the Child Benefit rules. Additionally, even if these circumstances were different, two people cannot receive Child Benefit for the same child.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or if you start making regular financial contributions towards his upkeep that are at least equal to the Child Benefit amount - your eligibility could be different, and you may wish to contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your situation.

reasonings: According to the Child Benefit rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. You normally qualify if you're responsible for a child, which means either: (1) you live with them, or (2) you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money. In Alex's case, he lives with his mum (not with you), and you are not contributing financially towards his upkeep. His mum is already claiming Child Benefit for him. Since Alex doesn't live with you and you're not making financial contributions towards his upkeep, you are not considered 'responsible' for Alex under the Child Benefit rules. Additionally, even if you were responsible, two people cannot get Child Benefit for the same child, and his mum is already claiming it. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is asking about Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. However, Alex lives with his mother who already claims Child Benefit for him. The claimant does not live with Alex and is not making financial contributions towards his upkeep. Under the rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and the claimant does not meet the criteria of being 'responsible' for Alex as defined by the regulations (living with the child or contributing at least as much as Child Benefit towards their upkeep). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible.

Since Alex doesn't live with you and you're not making financial contributions towards his upkeep, you are not considered 'responsible' for Alex under the Child Benefit rules. Additionally, even if you were responsible, two people cannot get Child Benefit for the same child, and his mum is already claiming it. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant does not live with Alex and is not making financial contributions towards his upkeep. Under the rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and the claimant does not meet the criteria of being 'responsible' for Alex as defined by the regulations (living with the child or contributing at least as much as Child Benefit towards their upkeep). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible.
